steps.title: Spot the chronological progression
steps.content: The passage moves from 1965 (the first syndicated strip by a Black cartoonist) to 1994 and 1996 (later syndicated strips). The blank connects these across time.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence. The blank needs a transition signaling that the 1994 and 1996 strips came after the 1965 milestone.
  • Choice A — "That is,": signals a restatement or clarification of the prior sentence, but the second sentence introduces new events, not a definition, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "Previously,": signals something happened before a reference point, but 1994 and 1996 come after 1965, so this reverses the timeline, failing. ✗
  • Choice C — "For example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim; the 1965 strip is the milestone, not a general claim that the later strips exemplify, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "Later,": signals temporal progression forward from 1965 to subsequent years, so fits. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
